revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=御井神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237| 祭神=[[木俣神|御井神]]| 社格=元郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| ...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=御井神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237| 祭神=[[木俣神|御井神]]| 社格=元郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=4月10日| 神事=| }}
'''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]御井神社であり、旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。
境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、[[鳥居]]は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない[[井戸]]があったという。
[[鎌倉時代]]の[[正応]]年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、[[安土桃山時代]]の[[天正]]年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。
[[江戸時代]]前期の[[正保]]年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。
[[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（[[1873年]]）には郷社となる。明治14年（[[1881年]]）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。
== 祭神 == *[[木俣神|御井神]]
== 所在地 == *岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237
== 交通 == *[[養老鉄道]][[養老鉄道養老線|養老線]][[美濃高田駅]]より約3km
{{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:養老町]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 交通 == *[[養老鉄道]][[養老鉄道養老線|養老線]][[美濃高田駅]]より約3km
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|300px]] |所在地=岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木俣神|御井神]] |社格=式内社（小）、元郷社 |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]御井神社であり、旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。
境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、[[鳥居]]は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない[[井戸]]があったという。
[[鎌倉時代]]の[[正応]]年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、[[安土桃山時代]]の[[天正]]年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。
[[江戸時代]]前期の[[正保]]年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。
[[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（[[1873年]]）には郷社となる。明治14年（[[1881年]]）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。
== 祭神 == *[[木俣神|御井神]]
== 交通 == *[[養老鉄道]][[養老鉄道養老線|養老線]][[美濃高田駅]]より約3km
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{nihongo|'''Mii Shrine'''|御井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Mii Shrine'''|御井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mii Shrine'''|御井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mii Shrine'''|御井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Mii Shrine'''|御井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木俣神|御井神]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）、旧[[郷社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=御井神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]御井神社であり、旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。 境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、[[鳥居]]は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない[[井戸]]があったという。 [[鎌倉時代]]の[[正応]]年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、[[安土桃山時代]]の[[天正]]年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。 [[江戸時代]]前期の[[正保]]年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。 [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（[[1873年]]）には郷社となる。明治14年（[[1881年]]）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。 == 祭神 == *[[木俣神|御井神]] == 交通 == *[[養老鉄道]][[養老鉄道養老線|養老線]][[美濃高田駅]]より約3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[御井神社 (養老町)]] to [[Mii Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Merging Japanese Wikipedia import with English content
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木俣神|御井神]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）、旧[[郷社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=御井神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]御井神社であり、旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。 境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、[[鳥居]]は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない[[井戸]]があったという。 [[鎌倉時代]]の[[正応]]年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、[[安土桃山時代]]の[[天正]]年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。 [[江戸時代]]前期の[[正保]]年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。 [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（[[1873年]]）には郷社となる。明治14年（[[1881年]]）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。 == 祭神 == *[[木俣神|御井神]] == 交通 == *[[養老鉄道]][[養老鉄道養老線|養老線]][[美濃高田駅]]より約3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

Province]] [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|御井神社 (養老町)|version=107307604|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[木俣神|御井神]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）、旧[[郷社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=御井神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]御井神社であり、旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は[[郷社]]。 境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、[[鳥居]]は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない[[井戸]]があったという。 [[鎌倉時代]]の[[正応]]年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、[[安土桃山時代]]の[[天正]]年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。 [[江戸時代]]前期の[[正保]]年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。 [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（[[1873年]]）には郷社となる。明治14年（[[1881年]]）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。 == 祭神 == *[[木俣神|御井神]] == 交通 == *[[養老鉄道]][[養老鉄道養老線|養老線]][[美濃高田駅]]より約3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|郷社}} |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=御井神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}御井神社であり、旧{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|社格}}は{{jalink|郷社}}。 境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、{{jalink|鳥居|Q234731}}は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない{{jalink|井戸|Q43483}}があったという。 {{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の{{jalink|正応|Q1327371}}年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、{{jalink|安土桃山時代|Q319531}}の{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}前期の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には郷社となる。明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。 == 祭神 == *{{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} == 交通 == *{{jalink|養老鉄道|Q11666892}}{{jalink|養老鉄道養老線|Q8062665|養老線}}{{jalink|美濃高田駅|Q11609279}}より約3km == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=御井神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}御井神社であり、旧{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、{{jalink|鳥居|Q234731}}は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない{{jalink|井戸|Q43483}}があったという。 {{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の{{jalink|正応|Q1327371}}年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、{{jalink|安土桃山時代|Q319531}}の{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}前期の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には郷社となる。明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|養老鉄道|Q11666892}}{{jalink|養老鉄道養老線|Q8062665|養老線}}{{jalink|美濃高田駅|Q11609279}}より約3km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=御井神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}御井神社であり、旧{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、{{jalink|鳥居|Q234731}}は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない{{jalink|井戸|Q43483}}があったという。 {{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の{{jalink|正応|Q1327371}}年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、{{jalink|安土桃山時代|Q319531}}の{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}前期の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には郷社となる。明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|養老鉄道|Q11666892}}{{jalink|養老鉄道養老線|Q8062665|養老線}}{{jalink|美濃高田駅|Q11609279}}より約3km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:need translation]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=御井神社 |画像=[[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}金屋237 |位置={{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=4月10日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=御井神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御井神社'''（みいじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}御井神社であり、旧{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 境内は春日神社と同一となっている。境内は同一であるが、{{jalink|鳥居|Q234731}}は御井神社と春日神社は別々になっている。社殿に向かって右側が御井神社である。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。「美濃国神名帳」に従五位下御井神の記載がある。元は現在地よりやや東にあり、境内には枯れることのない{{jalink|井戸|Q43483}}があったという。 {{jalink|鎌倉時代|Q236205}}の{{jalink|正応|Q1327371}}年間に洪水で大きな被害を受ける。再建されたが、{{jalink|安土桃山時代|Q319531}}の{{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}年間には戦乱で焼失し衰退する。 {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}前期の{{jalink|正保|Q1146192}}年間の洪水の後、近くの神明神社の地に遷座する。御井神社は神明神社の境内社となる。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）に御井神社を主社、神明神社を境内社に変更され、明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）には郷社となる。明治14年（{{jalink|1881年|Q7808}}）に春日神社の境内（現在地）に遷座する。 === 祭神 === *{{jalink|木俣神|Q11092833|御井神}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|養老鉄道|Q11666892}}{{jalink|養老鉄道養老線|Q8062665|養老線}}{{jalink|美濃高田駅|Q11609279}}より約3km === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みいしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{jalink|well|Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{jalink|Shōō|Q1327371}} era of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era of the {{jalink|Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} era of the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{jalink|Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279}} on the {{jalink|Yōrō Line|Q8062665|Yōrō Line}} of the {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{jalink|well|Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{jalink|Shōō|Q1327371}} era of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era of the {{jalink|Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} era of the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{jalink|Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279}} on the {{jalink|Yōrō Line|Q8062665|Yōrō Line}} of the {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{jalink|well|Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{jalink|Shōō|Q1327371}} era of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era of the {{jalink|Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} era of the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{jalink|Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279}} on the {{jalink|Yōrō Line|Q8062665|Yōrō Line}} of the {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{jalink|well|Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{jalink|Shōō|Q1327371}} era of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era of the {{jalink|Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} era of the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{jalink|Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279}} on the {{jalink|Yōrō Line|Q8062665|Yōrō Line}} of the {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor), former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yoro District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yoro Town|Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{jalink|torii|Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{jalink|well|Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{jalink|Shōō|Q1327371}} era of the {{jalink|Kamakura period|Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{jalink|Tenshō|Q1145058}} era of the {{jalink|Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{jalink|Shōhō|Q1146192}} era of the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{jalink|1881|Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{jalink|Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279}} on the {{jalink|Yōrō Line|Q8062665|Yōrō Line}} of the {{jalink|Yōrō Railway|Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miijinja}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]
