== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|WD=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|WD=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|WD=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|WD=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|WD=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|WD=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|WD=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|WD=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|WD=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|WD=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|qid=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|qid=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|qid=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|qid=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|qid=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|qid=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|qid=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|qid=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|qid=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|qid=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|qid=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2200005005113 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町金屋237', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:御井神社 (養老町)]] {{wikidata link|Q11488774}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Mii Shrine |Image = [[File:Mii shrine, Yoro.jpg|250px]]
Torii gate and worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|qid=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|qid=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Kanaya 237 |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|19|11.1|N|136|33|5.6|E|type:landmark_region:JP-21|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Annual festival = April 10 |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Mii Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mii Shrine''' (みいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yoro District|Q1155060|qid=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yoro Town|Q153814|qid=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}. It is the Mii Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|qid=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The shrine grounds are shared with Kasuga Shrine. Although the precincts are the same, the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} gates for Mii Shrine and Kasuga Shrine are separate. Facing the shrine buildings, the right-hand side belongs to Mii Shrine. === History === The date of establishment is unknown. The “Mino no Kuni Jinmyōchō” records the deity Mii-no-kami with the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Originally, the shrine stood slightly east of its present location, and it is said that within the grounds there was a {{ill|lt=well|Q43483|qid=Q43483|1=Well}} that never ran dry. During the {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|qid=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine suffered severe damage due to flooding. It was rebuilt, but during the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era of the {{ill|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|Q319531|qid=Q319531|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}, it was destroyed by warfare and fell into decline. After flooding in the {{ill|lt=Shōhō|Q1146192|qid=Q1146192|1=Shōhō}} era of the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was relocated to the grounds of the nearby Shinmei Shrine. Mii Shrine then became a subsidiary shrine within the precincts of Shinmei Shrine. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|qid=Q7717|1=1868}}), Mii Shrine was designated the main shrine and Shinmei Shrine was changed to a subsidiary shrine. In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was promoted to gōsha rank. In Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Kasuga Shrine (the present location). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kimata-no-kami|Q11092833|qid=Q11092833|lt=Mii-no-kami|1=Kimata-no-Kami}} === Access === * About 3 km from {{ill|lt=Mino-Takata Station|Q11609279|qid=Q11609279|1=Mino-Takada Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Line|Q8062665|qid=Q8062665|lt=Yōrō Line|1=Yōrō Railway Yōrō Line}} of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Railway|Q11666892|qid=Q11666892|1=Yoro Railway}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mii Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]
